name.COMMENT: Name: Arbitrary name to refer to this channel group (this information is not used in BIDS).

Reference.COMMENT: Reference: General description of the reference scheme used and (when applicable) of location of the reference electrode in the raw recordings (e.g., "left mastoid", "bipolar", "T01" for electrode with name T01, "intracranial electrode on top of a grid, not included with data", "upside down electrode").

name.COMMENT: Name: Label of the channel. The label must correspond to _electrodes.tsv name and all ieeg type channels are required to have a position.

units.COMMENT: Units: Physical unit of the value represented in this channel, e.g., V for Volt, specified according to the SI unit symbol and possibly prefix symbol (e.g., mV, μV).

low_cutoff.COMMENT: Low Cutoff: Frequencies used for the low pass filter applied to the channel in Hz. If no low pass filter was applied, use n/a. Note that anti-alias is a low pass filter, specify its frequencies here if applicable.

high_cutoff.COMMENT: High Cutoff: Frequencies used for the high pass filter applied to the channel in Hz. If no high pass filter applied, use n/a.

reference.COMMENT: Reference: Specification of the reference (e.g., "mastoid", "ElectrodeName01", "intracranial", "CAR", "other", "n/a"). If the channel is not an electrode channel (e.g., a microphone channel) use n/a

groups.COMMENT: Group: Which group of channels (grid/strip/seeg/depth) this channel belongs to. This is relevant because one group has one cable-bundle and noise can be shared. This can be a name or number.

description.COMMENT: Description: Brief free-text description of the channel, or other information of interest (e.g., position (e.g., "left lateral temporal surface", etc.).

notch.COMMENT: Notch Filter: Frequencies used for the notch filter applied to the channel, in Hz. If no notch filter applied, use n/a.

status.COMMENT: Status: Data quality observed on the channel (good/bad). A channel is considered bad if its data quality is compromised by excessive noise. Description of noise type SHOULD be provided in [status_description].

status_description.COMMENT: Status Description: Freeform text description of noise or artifact affecting data quality on the channel. It is meant to explain why the channel was declared bad in [status].
